posts.title: Glo2Facial vs. a Classic Facial: Which Is Right for You?
posts.excerpt: Both leave you glowing — but they work differently. Here's how to choose the right treatment for your skin and your goals.
posts.body:
Two of our most popular treatments both deliver a beautiful glow — but they get there in different ways. Here's how to know which one your skin is asking for.

A **classic facial** is the foundation of healthy skin: a deep cleanse, exfoliation, extractions if needed, and a nourishing mask tailored to your skin type. It's relaxing, restorative, and perfect for regular maintenance and keeping your skin balanced.

## So what's a Glo2Facial?

The **Glo2Facial** takes things a step further. It's an oxygenating treatment that uses customizable OxyPods to exfoliate, infuse, and boost circulation — triggering a fresh, lit-from-within glow you can see immediately, with zero downtime.

## Which should you book?

- **Choose a classic facial** for ongoing maintenance, relaxation, and balanced skin.
- **Choose a Glo2Facial** when you want maximum glow for an event, or a noticeable refresh.

Not sure? Book either one and we'll assess your skin in person and recommend the best plan for you.

posts.title: What to Expect From Your First Microchanneling Session
posts.excerpt: Curious about ProCell microchanneling? Here's exactly what happens, what it feels like, and how to prep for the best results.
posts.body:
Microchanneling sounds intense — but it's one of the most effective, comfortable ways to renew your skin. Here's what your first session actually looks like.

ProCell microchanneling works by creating thousands of tiny micro-channels in the surface of the skin. Your body reads these as a signal to repair, kicking off its natural healing response and ramping up fresh collagen and elastin.

## What it feels like

Most clients describe it as a light vibration with mild pressure. We use serums and take care to keep you comfortable throughout. A typical session takes under an hour.

## How to prep

- Arrive with clean skin, no makeup if possible.
- Avoid retinol and exfoliating acids for a few days beforehand.
- Stay hydrated and skip sun exposure before and after.

Afterward your skin may look lightly flushed for a day. Results build over the following weeks as new collagen forms — and they compound with a series of sessions.

posts.title: 5 Habits That Keep Your Skin Glowing Between Facials
posts.excerpt: Your at-home routine matters just as much as your treatments. These simple habits help your glow last longer.
posts.body:
Treatments do the heavy lifting — but what you do at home is what makes the results last. Five habits worth building.

## 1. Wear SPF every single day

Sun exposure is the number one cause of premature aging. Daily broad-spectrum SPF protects everything your treatments are working to improve.

## 2. Keep your routine simple and consistent

A gentle cleanser, a targeted serum, and a good moisturizer used consistently beats a shelf of products used randomly.

## 3. Hydrate from the inside

Glowing skin starts with hydration. Drink water throughout the day — your skin barrier will thank you.

## 4. Don't over-exfoliate

More isn't better. Over-exfoliating damages your skin barrier. Let your treatments handle the deep work.

## 5. Book regular maintenance

Skin renews on a cycle. Regular facials keep you ahead of congestion, dullness, and texture before they build up.
